reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures the informal problem: the matrix A over Fin n × Fin n has entries 1/min(i+1, j+1) (computed in ℚ and coerced to ℝ), which matches the informal 1/min(i,j) for 1 ≤ i,j ≤ n indexing (since i.1 ranges in {0,...,n-1}, so i.1+1 ranges in {1,...,n}). The solution `(-1)^(n-1) / ((n-1)! · n!)` is the correct Putnam 2014 A2 answer (verified at small cases n=1,2,3). The `npos : n > 0` hypothesis surfaces the standard implicit convention that "n × n matrix" means n ≥ 1, which is the natural reading of the informal statement. The conclusion `A.det = putnam_2014_a2_solution n` directly asks for the determinant computation as specified.
